title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A production platform response team. The team needs a hermetic bisect harness, isolated parallel worktrees, explicit remote mappings, lease-safe publication, and cleanup that cannot overwrite unseen work. Planned artifact: Deliver Parallel Regression Response Environment with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Case G19 is a project in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-force-with-lease-atomic-kimna7" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Force With Lease Atomic into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-force-with-lease-atomic-kimna7
# scenario-key: trace-kimna7 probe-7anmik boundary-mna7ki owner-kimna7x fault-kimna7z
# before-ref: main=a11ceki candidate=b00b1a7
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11ceki candidate=c0ffee7
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Force With Lease Atomic structural check is only one gate. distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.

hints: Name the Gita Force With Lease Atomic precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G27 is a project in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push. Competency probe: Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Bare --force-with-lease is a permanent guarantee that unseen remote work cannot be lost.
